title: Fix failing unit tests
body:
This PR addresses unit test failures introduced around the internal concurrency waiters and call handling. The focus is to stabilize the wake-up, cancellation, and timeout paths exercised by AsyncWaiter/SyncWaiter in tests like test_waiter_repr, test_async_waiter*, test_sync_waiter*, and related cancellation scenarios. Changes target the internal concurrency layer to ensure:
- Early submissions are reliably re-seeded into the event loop when the loop becomes available.
- Cancellation/timeouts propagate promptly and deterministically through futures and callbacks.
- Waiting callbacks and done-callbacks are executed in the correct thread/loop context without deadlocks or race conditions.

No public API surface changes; the changes are purely to fix flaky test behavior and ensure deterministic test outcomes across the waiters tests and related call scheduling.

step_plan.summary: This PR stabilizes internal concurrency waiters (AsyncWaiter/SyncWaiter) and the Call/Future deadline/cancellation machinery to fix flaky unit tests by making early submissions, wake-ups, and cancellation paths deterministic without altering public APIs.

steps.operation: Initialize robust guards in AsyncWaiter.__init__ to support early submissions and safe loop/queue binding
steps.reason: To ensure internal state (queue, loop reference, early_submissions) is consistently prepared before wait() is invoked, preventing race when waiter is created outside loop
steps.side_effects: Potentially affects tests that instantiate waiters outside loop; minor performance overhead; may require tests to account for explicit state initialization

steps.operation: Strengthen AsyncWaiter.wait to robustly re-seed early_submissions into the owning loop and propagate cancellation signals
steps.reason: Early submissions must be enqueued when loop becomes available; cancellation should unblock promptly
steps.side_effects: Could alter ordering of callbacks; ensure deterministic wakeups; may require tests to adapt to timing expectations

steps.operation: Refine AsyncWaiter._handle_waiting_callbacks to guarantee proper gathering and interleaving of callbacks across threads/loop
steps.reason: Make sure waiting callbacks are executed in proper context even under cancellation/timeouts; avoid deadlocks
steps.side_effects: May impact tests around callback ordering; ensure cross-thread coordination remains deterministic

steps.operation: Refine AsyncWaiter._handle_done_callbacks to ensure cancellation-sensitive execution and correct lifecycle of done callbacks
steps.reason: Ensure done callbacks fire reliably, respect cancellation, and run in expected loop, preventing missed signals or deadlocks
steps.side_effects: Tests asserting done callback invocation order may need to adapt; keeps public API unchanged

steps.operation: Tighten Call.run scheduling to respect per-context event loop and avoid misrouted coroutines under test timing
steps.reason: Guarantee that the execution path uses the correct loop and context in both sync and async paths; reduce race conditions with cancellations
steps.side_effects: Potential changes in how tasks are scheduled; may affect tests relying on exact scheduling; ensure compatibility with existing public APIs

steps.operation: Strengthen Future.enforce_async_deadline with robust cancellation callbacks alignment for async paths
steps.reason: Ensure that async deadlines reliably cancel and trigger cancellation callbacks, reducing flaky timeout races
steps.side_effects: Tests exercising async deadlines should be more deterministic; potential changes to timing semantics across tests

steps.operation: Strengthen Future.enforce_sync_deadline with robust cancellation callbacks alignment for sync paths
steps.reason: Mirror the async deadline handling for sync path to ensure consistent behavior and deterministic tests
steps.side_effects: Similar to async, may adjust test timing expectations; ensure no public API changes
